source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Ship Shoal 198-J Platform Explosion

actions_taken: Injured person transported to Thibodeaux Regional Medical Center for CAT scan
Personnel retraining proposed on hazard analysis and purging requirements
event.summary: On July 16, 2017, at approximately 4:30 pm, a Waukesha Reciprocating Compressor on the Ship Shoal 198-J platform experienced an internal explosion during start-up operations. The compressor had undergone maintenance on July 12-15, 2017, during which internal components were exposed to atmosphere. During restart, the compressor was not purged or loaded before running idle for approximately ten minutes with the blowdown valve open. An LEL mixture of air and natural gas ignited within the process piping, causing the explosion and damaging the 3rd stage cooler and associated piping. The compressor mechanic experienced migraines and dizziness five days later. Probable causes were identified as failure to purge and inadequate job safety analysis.
recommendations: Retrain all personnel on recognition of hazard analysis and proper system purging requirements following any repairs that expose combustible equipment to atmospheric conditions
Develop and implement a general purging procedure
Improve employee competency testing on purging techniques
Enhance hazard analysis processes to identify purging as a key step in job safety analysis
key_phrases: lack of detail[ed] understanding of proper purging techniques

hazards.name: Flammable gas mixture (air and natural gas)
Atmospheric exposure of internal compressor components

threats.name: Formation of LEL mixture in process piping during idle operation
Failure to purge compressor after maintenance exposure
Blowdown valve left open allowing continuous air ingress

consequences.name: Structural damage to 3rd stage cooler and piping

controls.name: Purging procedure for post-maintenance compressor start-up
controls.barrier_role: Prevent formation of flammable mixture by removing air from process
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent hazardous condition

supporting_text: the compressor was restarted without being purged or loaded
air from the bleed points [was] not properly purged[,] which allowed a continuous source of air into the process and components
purging was not even recognized as a key step in the job safety analysis (JSA)
At the time of the incident, Renaissance had no general purging procedure in place

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for compressor maintenance and start-up
controls.barrier_role: Identify and communicate critical safety steps including purging
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to ensure hazards are identified and communicated

supporting_text: purging was not even recognized as a key step in the job safety analysis (JSA)

controls.name: Blowdown valve management during idle operation
controls.barrier_role: Prevent air ingress into process during idle operation
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to isolate process from atmosphere

supporting_text: the engine ran in idle for approximately ten minutes with the blowdown valve (BDV) in the open position
air from the bleed points [was] not properly purged[,] which allowed a continuous source of air into the process and components

controls.name: Employee competency testing and training on purging techniques
controls.barrier_role: Ensure personnel understand and can execute proper purging procedures
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to ensure human capability

supporting_text: lack of detail[ed] understanding of proper purging techniques
Retrain all personnel on recognition of hazard analysis and proper system purging requirements
